free_text: Complete sets of polarization transfer observables for
the 208Pb(p,n) reaction at 296MeV and Gamow-Teller and
spin-dipole strengths for 208Pb
Department of Physics
RIKEN Nishina Center for Accelerator-Based
Science
Research Center for Nuclear Physics
Department of Applied Physics
Cyclotron and Radioisotope Center
Department of Physics
Beam intensity: 30 and 500 nA for cross-section and
polarization transfer measurements, respectively.
POL-BM: Typical magnitude of beam
polarization.
- Target enrichment: The enrichment of 208Pb target is
greater than and equal to 99%
- Chemical-form of target is element.
- Physical-form of target is solid.
- Target-thickness is 634 mg/cm**2.
- Target is self supported. 208Pb target located in a
beam swinger magnet.
To measure neutron energy
To measure neutron polarization
Research Center for Nuclear Physics
(RCNP)
- Calibration: 7Li(p,n)7Be(g.s+0.43 MeV) reaction at
theta=0 degree.
- Efficiency: 0.043+-0.002 was the resulting
efficiency.
- The polarization transfer observables, DNN and DLL
were measured using old neutron detector and
polarimeter (NPOL2).
Neutrons were measured by the NPOL3 system,
which consists of three planes of neutron
detectors. Each of the first two planes
consists of 10 sets of one-dimensional
position-sensitive plastic scintillators
(BC408). The last plane is a two-dimensional
position-sensitive plastic scintillator.
1. DWIA (Distorted wave impulse approximation)
2. Random phase approximation
V.D
Received by e-mail from T.Wakasa
ANG is polar angle (lab.) between beam and neutron
